title: Portable Context Control Plane — User Constraints Input
description: Schema for the --constraints CLI input file. Operator-supplied explicit component inclusion/exclusion constraints. Class B input — if absent or malformed: treat as no constraints (empty arrays); emit user_constraints_missing warning only if the field was present but malformed. MVP constraints are always operator-supplied high-trust inputs — no constraintTrustLevel field (safe-defer 5-Q5). Safety override is handled by planner/conflict resolver logic, not this input schema: a neverInclude constraint on a safety-critical component is overridden by safety rules and logged as safety_override. constraintSource is an informational string identifying the constraint origin. Canonical owners: docs/06 §2.8; docs/11 §4.1; docs/12 §3.8.
$comment: Pass 4.9C-2B — Batch B. No code. No runtime. No provider/model calls. No docs edited. Non-MVP excluded: constraintTrustLevel (safe-defer 5-Q5). Safety override is conflict resolver / planner logic, not schema. Canonical sources: docs/06 §2.8, docs/11 §4.1, docs/12 §3.8.

alwaysInclude.title: Always include component IDs
alwaysInclude.description: Component IDs that must be included in the plan regardless of selector output. Processed by the Conflict Resolver at user_constraint_include priority (priority 2). A component in alwaysInclude that is ALSO safety-critical is already hard-protected — the constraint is redundant but not an error. These are registry component ID strings; unknown IDs produce reference_unknown SelectionDecision records (not active_id_unknown warnings).

neverInclude.title: Never include component IDs
neverInclude.description: Component IDs that must be excluded from the plan regardless of selector output. Safety override rule: if a neverInclude constraint conflicts with a hard-protection rule (retainPolicy: safety_critical, omissionPolicy: never, riskLevel: critical), safety wins — the constraint is overridden and logged as safety_override in the Conflict Resolver trace. This override is planner/conflict resolver logic, not enforced at schema validation boundary. These are registry component ID strings.

constraintSource.title: Constraint source
constraintSource.description: Informational string identifying the origin of these constraints. Used for traceability in planning warnings and trace entries. In MVP, always operator-supplied: example values are 'operator_cli' or 'session_policy'. Not interpreted by selector logic — audit/trace use only. Non-MVP trust-level differentiation (constraintTrustLevel) is excluded per safe-defer 5-Q5.
